fieldGuide: 1️⃣ 500 km + two major stops = schedule risk—build slack or film failure honestly.
2️⃣ Horseshoe Bend: railings end; wind gusts at rim—never turn your back to load a drone case.
3️⃣ Antelope Canyon: tour slots tie to sun angle—late beams are physics, not ‘luck.’
4️⃣ Flash flood protocol in slots: upstream rain can arrive before local sky looks bad—ask guides, film the safety briefing.
5️⃣ Bryce rim: frost-wedging geology—temperature swing after sunset can ice footpaths.
6️⃣ Hydration math: publish your liter count if you teach desert riding—otherwise it’s theater.
13.hook: Epic on paper is logistics in reality—today you either manage energy or the desert invoices you.
13.storyArc: Pre-dawn departure timestamp. Page stops as timeboxed modules. Transit legs as map + fuel receipts. Bryce sunset: exposure bracket or HDR decision stated. Night camp: Milky Way settings on screen.
talkingPoints: Horseshoe Bend incision meander—river vs rock timescales.
Antelope Canyon flood hydraulics—speed of wall of water.
Lake Powell storage politics—one neutral sentence, cited.
Hoodoo formation—frost wedging without fairy tales.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ UT-12 hogsback: crosswind from either valley—scan both before passing.
2️⃣ Capitol Reef water: fill at every reliable tap—next dry gap is real.
3️⃣ I-70 Green River–Salina: 110 miles no services—fuel arithmetic on camera.
4️⃣ Arches timed entry: screenshot reservation; gate audio often unusable—lav mic.
5️⃣ Delicate Arch hike: carry 2L+ in summer—heat illness looks like ‘dramatic collapse’ online; it’s medicine.
6️⃣ Moab slickrock: tire pressures off-road vs highway—state both numbers.
14.hook: America’s ‘most scenic highway’ is still a road with crossfalls, thermals, and distracted drivers.
14.storyArc: UT-12 as geological transect (five zones, named). I-70 void as psychological interval. Moab arrival: land use (tourism vs mining ghost). Arches: reservation system as carrying capacity tool.
talkingPoints: National Geographic claims—what metric they used.
Arch collapse events—erosion continues on human timescales.
I-70 desolation economics—why exits died.
Thelma & Louise location ethics—stunt geography vs visitor safety.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Zero day is production reset: batteries, media offload, lens clean, gimbal calibration—show the boring work.
2️⃣ Delicate Arch dawn: headlamp redundancy; slickrock navigation errors injure people yearly.
3️⃣ Dead Horse Point: 600 m exposure—tripod foot placement beats selfie sticks.
4️⃣ Edward Abbey mention: quote short, cite Desert Solitaire—don’t cosplay misanthropy.
5️⃣ Town afternoon: record audio room tone for later ADR matching.
6️⃣ Still evening: let silence run 10s on timeline—documentary pacing lesson.
15.hook: Stillness isn’t laziness—it’s when the brain indexes trauma, joy, and mileage.
15.storyArc: Dawn hike as technical sequence (footing, water, sun angle). Overlook geometry. Town logistics. Evening reflection with zero B-roll—face + sound + honest words.
talkingPoints: Why rest days reduce crash risk statistically.
Delicate Arch naming history—fact check settlers vs marketing.
Dead Horse legend—separate myth from hydrology.
Moab outdoor industrial scale—carrying capacity debate.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Virgin River Gorge: downdrafts and debris in spring—visor down through construction zones.
2️⃣ Zion tunnel: measure handlebar width; escort rules are width-based, not ‘feeling.’
3️⃣ BLM dispersed: camp 300 ft from water where required; film the rule sign.
4️⃣ St. George heat: morning ride blocks beat afternoon hero segments.
5️⃣ Dinosaur tracks site: fragile matrix—tripods rubber feet only.
6️⃣ Audio: desert cicada beds vs wind—choose VO windows.
16.hook: Utah’s south is engineered verticality—tunnels, bridges, and blast cuts—not postcard softness.
16.storyArc: I-15 as controlled canyon flight. Zion as foreshadow (tomorrow’s heat). St. George as cultural layer (Mormon Dixie history, stated neutrally). BLM night as stars + fire discipline.
talkingPoints: Virgin River Gorge cost per mile—why politicians still cite it.
Zion tunnel 1930 spec vs modern ADV width.
BLM acreage mandate—multiple use in one sentence.
Mojave preview: wet-bulb temperature concept.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Pavement 65°C+: tire pressure rises—check cold, not at McDonald’s after lunch.
2️⃣ Hydration: electrolyte loss without food equals cramping—film what you actually eat.
3️⃣ Valley of Fire: midday heat stroke risk—shoot edges of day unless medically prepared.
4️⃣ Lane-splitting entering CA: cite CHP guidance year; don’t improvise legal advice.
5️⃣ Joshua tree range shift: climate science one-liner from USGS or peer summary.
6️⃣ Engine temp lights: pull off before the boil-over—teach warning signs on DR650 specifically if applicable.
17.hook: The Mojave isn’t testing your spirit—it’s testing your thermodynamics and your coolant.
17.storyArc: Heat metrics on screen (air, pavement estimate, engine). Nevada color section. California border as regulatory shift. Suburban approach as cognitive dissonance. Close: Pacific air mass in forecast.
talkingPoints: Radiation vs convection cooling on bikes.
Mojave endorheic basins—why ‘dry’ still floods.
Roy Rogers trivia vs Apple Valley sprawl facts.
Psychological threshold: inland vs coastal California.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ LA basin: filter lane-splitting through traffic law + survival—never teach recklessness.
2️⃣ Pacific first sight: exposure for white foam + dark water—zebra, don’t clip highlights.
3️⃣ Santa Monica Route 66 terminus: match Adrian midpoint math on screen—close the arc with numbers.
4️⃣ Big Sur: no cell = no upload; plan safety check-ins before Bixby.
5️⃣ Prewitt Ridge: high-clearance + dark = rollover risk—arrive with sun angle, walk the road first.
6️⃣ Cliff camp: stake for wind, not just rain; ocean downdrafts are real.
18.hook: The ocean isn’t the climax—it’s a hydrologic boundary you’ve been approaching for weeks.
18.storyArc: Freeway approach as systems test. Ocean reveal as controlled beat (pullout, tripod, audio). PCH as corridor geology. Big Sur as infrastructure fragility (slides). Ridge camp as exposure management + ethical filming neighbors.
talkingPoints: PCH prison labor history—one verifiable sentence.
Leo Carrillo coastal access law lineage.
Bixby bridge statics—span length vs height correctly.
CHP lane-filtering phrasing—quote, don’t paraphrase.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Marine layer: ND or wait—gray ocean reads flat without polarizer discipline.
2️⃣ San Andreas onshore: fault trace photography needs scale reference—people or bike.
3️⃣ 1906 damage at Point Arena—tie earthquake rupture length to felt area.
4️⃣ Lost Coast roads: landslide maintenance—check Caltrans before promising passage.
5️⃣ GPS coordinate insert: verify datum WGS84 on screen—map nerds will catch errors.
6️⃣ Fog audio: low-pass wind muff; dialogue windows short.
19.hook: The continent’s edge is also a plate boundary—film both the romantic and the geophysical.
19.storyArc: Northward transect of climate (temperature, dew point). Lighthouse as survey point + hazard light history. Fault walk as grounded science. Lost Coast as deliberate non-development.
talkingPoints: Hawaii great-circle distance—show map arc, not fake arrow.
Murder She Wrote location substitution—how TV exports false geography.
Mendocino homestead culture—succinct, respectful.
Rider gear for 10°C sea air vs desert yesterday.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Redwoods: vertical shots need nodal pan or parallax ruins scale—use person+bike reference.
2️⃣ Fog drip ecosystem—record drips on leaves with close mic; it’s the science beat.
3️⃣ Elk on road: rut season aggression—never corner animals for frame.
4️⃣ Avenue of the Giants: tourist traffic pulse—shoot at corridor edges.
5️⃣ Hyperion: don’t publish hidden coords—ethical documentary standard.
6️⃣ Low light under canopy: ISO noise vs blur—pick poison, explain choice.
20.hook: These trees record centuries in cambium—your episode should respect slower timescales than TikTok.
20.storyArc: Canopy closure as light falloff graph. Touch + sound (bark, drip). Elk as megafauna hazard. Night silence as data (dB if possible).
talkingPoints: Coast redwood vs giant sequoia—range and volume facts.
Fire-resistant bark chemistry—tannins, succinct.
Old-growth policy—one neutral policy sentence.
Tourism load on US-101—peak hour strategy.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Border climb on US-199: cold tires + tight curves—first 10 minutes are crash window.
2️⃣ Crater Lake detour: verify rim road open; snow bergs in June block lanes.
3️⃣ Blue water: polarization helps; altitude sun burns skin faster—SPF on camera.
4️⃣ Ghost towns: structure safety—no entering collapsed structures for ‘ruin porn.’
5️⃣ Timber towns: unemployment narratives—cite one current statistic source year.
6️⃣ Westfir audio: river white noise for bed—levels consistent.
21.hook: Oregon is where the ice sheets split—your road is either driftless or planed flat. Read the ground.
21.storyArc: Border transition as species change (Douglas fir density). Optional Crater Lake module with go/no-go honesty. Timber valleys as economic geography. Arrival small town as quiet denouement.
talkingPoints: Mazama collapse scale—compare to St. Helens correctly.
Klamath oral history—attribute, don’t appropriate.
Oregon ghost town count—define ‘recognized’ source.
Rain shadow number—cite inches west vs east of Cascades.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Gorge wind: sport bikes tuck; ADV presents sail area—body English fundamentals.
2️⃣ Multnomah permit system: screenshot; overcrowding closures are real.
3️⃣ Missoula floods: quote peak discharge order of magnitude—don’t invent ‘biggest ever’ without era.
4️⃣ Bridge of the Gods: crosswalk etiquette—pedestrian right-of-way on busy span.
5️⃣ Celilo loss: submerged falls—use archival respectfully, cite Army Corps timeline.
6️⃣ Drone near falls: FAA + park rules—film signage.
22.hook: This gorge was carved by catastrophic outburst floods—your wind buffeting is trivial physics by comparison.
22.storyArc: Westfir descent to Columbia width reveal. Historic Highway stonework as craft. Wind sports as flow visualization. Goldendale plateau as rain-shadow exit.
talkingPoints: Missoula flood count (~40) and chronology window.
Multnomah flow seasonality.
Hood River wind funnel math—simple.
Landslide dam story for Bridge of Gods—separate legend from geology.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Urban riding Portland/Seattle: track stand vs foot down—choose safety over cool.
2️⃣ Pike Place audio: peak SPL—manual gain, backup lav.
3️⃣ St. Helens view: explain truncated summit vs pre-1980 profile—use overlay.
4️⃣ Tech history lists: date each founding—Amazon 1994 ≠ Microsoft 1975.
5️⃣ Rain shadow transition eastbound memory—callback to day 22.
6️⃣ Ferry math if used: walk-on vs bike fare on screen.
23.hook: Cascadia isn’t vibes—it’s basalt floods, venture capital, and volcanoes wearing off.
23.storyArc: Portland cultural module (books, beer) with numbers. I-5 as spine. Seattle skyline + market as sensory overload. Volcano glimpse as geologic callback.
talkingPoints: St. Helens energy release vs Hiroshima analog—use USGS-approved comparison or skip.
Pike Place continuity since 1907—fact.
Portland brewery per capita—define city limits.
Grunge as economic history—not costume.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ SR-20 opening dates: screenshot WSDOT; snow walls beside lane mean meltwater + debris.
2️⃣ Washington Pass: ice patches in shade—tire compound matters.
3️⃣ Avalanche paths overhead—spring warming increases release risk; don’t stop under chutes.
4️⃣ Winthrop western theme: 1972 design decision—cite local history archive.
5️⃣ Camp elevation: note sleeping bag rating vs actual low.
6️⃣ Glacier retreat photo pair—use dated USGS if comparing.
24.hook: This highway is seasonal infrastructure—if it’s open, you’re borrowing time from winter.
24.storyArc: Check passes online—show screen. Climb as temperature drop curve. Liberty Bell as rock type lesson. Winthrop as deliberate aesthetic. Camp under peaks with stated elevation.
talkingPoints: North Cascade glacier count vs Alps comparison—careful with units.
Winthrop redesign—tourism economics.
East-west cultural split—one balanced sentence.
Motorcycle season touring—tire temp in shade.
